name: ttlcache
description: A standard-library-only, concurrency-safe TTL key-value cache. Set(k,v,ttl) stores a value with a time-to-live; Get(k) returns (value, ok) and reports ok==false for missing or expired entries, deleting expired entries lazily on read. A sync.Mutex guards a map[string]item; expiry uses time.Time deadlines (ttl<=0 means never expire). The clock is an injectable field so tests are deterministic without real sleeps. Verified green with gofmt, go build, go vet, go test, and go test -race on Go 1.25.3 against a `go 1.23` module.

maintain.request: Lazy expiry only reclaims memory for keys that are read again, so keys that are written once and never read leak forever. Add a DeleteExpired() int method that proactively sweeps and removes every expired entry and returns the number of entries it removed, so callers can run it periodically to bound memory.

review.bug: Get no longer acquires c.mu, so it reads from (and deletes expired entries out of) the shared items map without synchronization, creating a data race with concurrent Set/Get/DeleteExpired calls.
keywords: get does not acquire the mutex
delete from map without holding the lock
